CortexVisionCoreTests/versionIsDefined().functional: Application reports a valid version string so users and logs can identify the running version
CortexVisionCoreTests/versionIsDefined().technical: CortexVision.version returns a non-empty string in semantic version format (X.Y.Z)
CortexVisionCoreTests/versionIsDefined().expectedOutput: Non-empty string containing at least one dot (e.g. "0.1.0")

CaptureModeTests/allModesHaveLabel().functional: Each capture mode displays a descriptive label in the toolbar
CaptureModeTests/allModesHaveLabel().technical: CaptureMode.allCases each return a non-empty label string

CaptureModeTests/allModesHaveSystemImage.functional: Each capture mode displays an icon in the toolbar using SF Symbols
CaptureModeTests/allModesHaveSystemImage.technical: CaptureMode.allCases each return a valid SF Symbol name string

CaptureModeTests/threeModes().functional: The app offers exactly three capture modes: window, region, scrolling

CaptureModeTests/uniqueIdentifiers().functional: Toolbar can distinguish between modes for correct selection state
CaptureModeTests/uniqueIdentifiers().technical: CaptureMode.id values are unique across all cases

CaptureStateTests/idleStatusText().functional: Status bar shows 'No capture' when app has not captured anything

CaptureStateTests/capturingStatusText().functional: Status bar indicates capture is in progress

CaptureStateTests/capturedStatusText().functional: After capture, status bar shows image dimensions

CaptureStateTests/analyzedStatusText().functional: After analysis, status bar shows word and figure counts
CaptureStateTests/analyzedStatusText().technical: CaptureState.analyzed.statusText contains word and figure counts

CaptureStateTests/errorStatusText().functional: Status bar shows error description when something goes wrong

ProtocolTests/captureProviderExists().functional: System can accept any capture implementation via protocol abstraction
ProtocolTests/captureProviderExists().technical: CaptureProvider protocol compiles and can be referenced as existential type

ProtocolTests/scrollCaptureProviderExist.functional: System can accept any scroll capture implementation via protocol abstraction
ProtocolTests/scrollCaptureProviderExist.technical: ScrollCaptureProvider protocol compiles and can be referenced

ProtocolTests/exportDestinationExists().functional: System can accept any export implementation via protocol abstraction
ProtocolTests/exportDestinationExists().technical: ExportDestination protocol compiles and can be referenced

ProtocolTests/permissionManagerExists().functional: System can accept any permission manager implementation via protocol
ProtocolTests/permissionManagerExists().technical: PermissionManager protocol compiles and can be referenced

ProtocolTests/captureResultConstruction(.functional: A capture result carries the image, target and timestamp
ProtocolTests/captureResultConstruction(.technical: CaptureResult init accepts CGImage, CaptureTarget, Date

ProtocolTests/exportConfigurationURLs().functional: Export knows where to write markdown and figure files
ProtocolTests/exportConfigurationURLs().technical: ExportConfiguration.markdownURL and figuresDirectoryURL are derived correctly
ProtocolTests/exportConfigurationURLs().expectedOutput: markdownURL ends with capture.md, figuresDirectoryURL ends with figures

ProtocolTests/scrollProgressEstimation().functional: During scrolling capture, user sees progress percentage

ProtocolTests/scrollProgressUnknownTotal.functional: Progress is nil when total frames is unknown during scrolling capture
ProtocolTests/scrollProgressUnknownTotal.technical: ScrollCaptureProgress.estimatedProgress returns nil when total is nil

ProtocolTests/permissionStatusCases().functional: System can represent all possible OS permission states
ProtocolTests/permissionStatusCases().technical: PermissionStatus has granted, denied, notDetermined, restricted cases
